name: Torre de Ardèvol and Castillo de Pinós — Circular Route in Solsonès
name_original: Torre de Ardèvol y Castillo de Pinós — Ruta circular al Solsonès
region_intro: Hiking around Pinós, located in Catalonia, offers a diverse landscape for outdoor exploration. The region is characterized by rolling hills, historical sites such as castles and sanctuaries, and natural features like the Salt Mountain. Trails often traverse forested areas and open countryside, providing varied terrain for hikers. The area includes the geographical center of Catalonia, adding a unique point of interest to many routes. Best hiking trails around Pinós The most popular hiking route is Santuari del Miracle to Bassa de l'Alzina Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of natural paths and historical points of interest. Another top favourite among local hikers is Torre de Ardèvol and Castillo de Pinós — Circular Route in Solsonès, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This circular route explores historical towers and the castle of Pinós, offering views of the Solsonès region. Local hikers also love the Sanctuary of Pinós – Geographical Center of Catalonia loop from Pinós, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail leading through the area surrounding the Sanctuary of Pinós and the geographical center of Catalonia, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Hiking around Pinós is defined by historical landmarks, varied forest paths, and open rural landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate trails. The routes in Pinós are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from around 60 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Pinós's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I find around Pinós?
A: Pinós offers a diverse range of hiking trails, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The landscape features rolling hills, forested areas, and open countryside. You'll find paths leading to historical sites like castles and sanctuaries, as well as natural wonders such as the Salt Mountain. There are over 40 routes available, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Pinós?
A: Yes, Pinós has several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38176004" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Cardona loop from Cardona&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 2.9 km route that takes less than an hour to complete, offering a gentle introduction to the area's beauty.

Q: What are some notable historical sites or landmarks to see while hiking in Pinós?
A: The Pinós region is rich in history. You can explore various castles and religious buildings. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4925019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellvell de Olius&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070943" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelltallat Castle (astronomical observatory)&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Pinós&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails incorporate these historical points of interest, offering a blend of nature and culture.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Pinós?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Pinós. A great example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938240611/torre-de-ardevol-and-castillo-de-pinos-circular-route-in-solsones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Ardèvol and Castillo de Pinós — Circular Route in Solsonès&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to explore historical towers and the castle without retracing your steps. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14951669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Pinós – Geographical Center of Catalonia loop from Pinós&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through significant regional landmarks.

Q: What is the best season for hiking in Pinós?
A: Pinós offers enjoyable hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery, with wildflowers in spring and vibrant foliage in autumn. Summers can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable, while winters are typically mild but can have cooler temperatures at higher elevations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Pinós?
A: The trails in Pinós are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from nearly 60 reviews. Hikers often praise the region's varied terrain, the blend of natural paths with historical sites, and the overall peaceful atmosphere. The opportunity to visit landmarks like the geographical center of Catalonia is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any trails that feature unique natural attractions like the Salt Mountain?
A: Yes, you can explore trails that lead to or near the unique geological formation of the Salt Mountain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14951694" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salt Mountain loop from Cardona&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.2 km route that allows you to experience this distinctive natural wonder.

Q: Are there good viewpoints along the hiking trails in Pinós?
A: Many trails in Pinós traverse rolling hills and open countryside, offering scenic views of the Solsonès region and the surrounding Catalan landscape. Routes that pass by historical towers and castles, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e938240611/torre-de-ardevol-and-castillo-de-pinos-circular-route-in-solsones" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Ardèvol and Castillo de Pinós — Circular Route in Solsonès&lt;/a&gt;, often provide excellent vantage points.

Q: How can I find less crowded hiking trails near Pinós?
A: While Pinós is generally less frequented than some major tourist areas, to find quieter trails, consider exploring some of the lesser-known routes or hiking during off-peak hours (early mornings or weekdays). Komoot offers a total of over 40 routes, so there are many options beyond the most popular ones to discover peaceful natural settings.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Pinós during autumn?
A: For autumn hiking in Pinós, it's best to dress in layers. Temperatures can vary, so a moisture-wicking base layer, an insulating mid-layer (like a fleece), and a waterproof/windproof outer shell are recommended. Comfortable hiking boots with good ankle support are essential, and consider bringing a hat and gloves for cooler mornings or evenings.

Q: Are there places to eat or have a coffee after hiking in Pinós?
A: While specific cafes directly on every trailhead might be limited, the nearby settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5070749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Solsona&lt;/a&gt; offers various options for refreshments and meals. The area around the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5069759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Pinós&lt;/a&gt; might also have facilities for visitors.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads in Pinós?
A: Public transport options directly to every trailhead in the rural Pinós region might be limited. It is generally recommended to have a car for convenient access to the various starting points of the hiking routes. However, some larger nearby towns like Solsona may have bus connections, from which you might need to arrange further transport.
tips: Magnificent tower documented in the year 986, and which has now been remodeled. It is in the center of Ardèvol and the municipality of Pinós, next to the church of the same name. We can ask for the keys at the bar in front.

The Sanctuary of Pinós is located at an altitude of 904 meters, which offers a magnificent panoramic view of the surrounding lands. The sanctuary was built by the Templars in 1312, but it soon passed into the hands of the Hospitallers.

We will find a compass rose carved into a stone, which indicates that this point is the geographical center of Catalonia. At an altitude of 904 meters and from where we have splendid views of the Pyrenees, Collsacabra and Montseny.
